The Beginning

Every dish started
a conversation.

It started simply. A plate of food, a table of people, and someone saying — "This is exactly what I needed." Not once. Not twice. But enough times that it stopped feeling like coincidence.

Travelers who tasted the food stayed longer than they planned. Friends gathered around a meal and left with a memory. There was something in the spice, in the warmth, that made people feel genuinely at home — even far from it.

"You have to do this for real. You can't just keep this to yourself."
That's what people kept saying. And eventually, Raghav listened.

"Some kitchens are built by capital.
This one is being built by people."

People who came for a meal and stayed for the memory.

Raghav has been feeding people the way only someone who genuinely loves it can — with intention, instinct, and care no recipe teaches. From hostel kitchens to cooking collaboratively with guests from around the world, food has always been the through-line.

Now it's time to build something real. A cloud kitchen. Not a franchise — a personal, soulful setup that carries the memory of every meal it inspired. Phase One is the proper foundation: equipment, space, licensing, everything to make this sustainable.
